threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'banger' instead of a 'sausage roll'—the locals will spot that in a heartbeat.
Saw a guy on the 35 last night talking to an empty seat like it was his best mate. Brixton after dark never disappoints.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a kid ride their bike past a guy selling homemade hummus out of a cardboard box—nothing says Neukölln like that mashup.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people casually sipping coffee in parks while their dogs run free, ignoring the leash laws like it's a form of public art.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they casually reference the ‘BART Meltdown’ like it’s just another Tuesday. The rest just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy jamming on a ukulele while the whole bus was stuck in traffic. Felt like a mini concert right there in the Tenderloin. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think paella is our thing, but on the daily, it's all about el plat de llenties and a good coca recomenda from the local forns.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still look for the escalators at Diagonal; locals know the stairs are quicker after years of dodging tourists.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look bewildered when someone offers you 'butter biscuits' at the local café, instead of just cookies. That's a rookie mistake!
Caught a whiff of jollof rice on the 35 last night. Some bloke was having a full-on feast at the back. It's a vibe you just can't get on the Tube.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was local? Watching the bus driver negotiate with a flock of geese blocking the road on Hermannstraße.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a Späti with a full craft beer fridge and handmade vegan kebabs. It's a late-night local treasure.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they’ve navigated the BART delays and still make it to Tacolicious before the lunch rush. That's real dedication.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the late-night crowd from the back of a 38 Geary. Surprise duet with a busker on the sidewalk. Classic SF vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals are at the parks on weekends. Meanwhile, many of us are tucked away in our kitchens, perfecting our Sunday paella.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the new ones by how they stare at the metro map like it's a treasure map—real locals know it's just a game of patience.
